india_trends.title: Stocks to buy or sell: Osho Krishan of Angel One suggests buying Bharat Dynamics, Max Financial shares to buy - 7 May
india_trends.summary: The Indian stock market opened positively on May 7, with the BSE Sensex up 380.72 points and the Nifty 50 rising 67.55 points. Market breadth was positive, led by gains in various sectors, while volatility in crude oil prices moderated overall gains.

india_trends.title: Paytm shares jump over 6% after Q4 profit, strong revenue growth
india_trends.summary: Consolidated revenue from operations rose 18.4 per cent y-o-y to ₹2,264 crore in Q4 FY26 from ₹1,912 crore a year earlier

india_trends.title: KPIT Technologies shares slide 4% as Q4 profit falls 33% YoY to Rs 163 crore
india_trends.summary: KPIT Technologies shares fell sharply on Thursday. The automotive software firm reported a 33% year-on-year decline in net profit for the March quarter. This occurred despite a healthy 12% rise in revenue. Operating expenses increased significantly. The company's board recommended a final dividend.

india_trends.title: Dividend alert! Last date to buy these 10 stocks before they go ex-record date. Do you own?
india_trends.summary: Investors have a final opportunity today to purchase shares of 10 companies before they go ex-record date for dividends tomorrow, May 8. Notable companies include Central Bank of India, Laurus Labs, NALCO, and Premier Energies, with dividend payouts varying across the listed firms.

india_trends.title: Polycab shares jump 6% as post-earnings target prices go up to Rs 10,500. Should you buy now?

Consolidated revenue rose 27% YoY, while EBITDA grew 13%. Analysts said the performance stood out despite geopolitical disruptions, weak Ma

india_trends.title: Shree Cement shares jump 4% even as Q4 profit drops 8%. Why Nomura, Elara, other brokerages remain bullish
india_trends.summary: Shree Cement shares rose nearly 4% despite an 8% dip in Q4 FY26 net profit to Rs 526 crore. Revenue, however, climbed 10% to Rs 6,1010 crore, with sales volumes showing significant year-on-year and quarter-on-quarter growth. Brokerages remain bullish, citing strong volume growth and dividend payouts

india_trends.title: Meesho shares surge over 7% after Q4 losses shrink sharply; Morgan Stanley raises target price
india_trends.summary: Meesho shares surged after the e-commerce platform posted an 88% YoY drop in Q4 losses to Rs 166 crore, while revenue jumped 47% to Rs 3,531 crore.
